teamObjective.explanation: The 3 of diamonds is the safest opening lead: it supports a potentially finishing partner, preserves stronger cards, and minimizes loss against opponents.

partnerBelief.summary: Player 2 is my partner and holds only 2 cards, so they may be trying to exit soon. A low diamond lead gives them the widest range of winning replies—any higher diamond or any trump—without forcing them to waste a high card.

opponentBelief.summary: Opponents have 3 cards each and no public plays yet, so they likely hold higher non-trump cards or trumps. They will try to win the trick cheaply; leading a 3 minimizes the value I surrender if they take it.

actionRationale.primaryReason: The 3 of diamonds is the safest opening lead: it supports a potentially finishing partner, preserves stronger cards, and minimizes loss against opponents.
